funding_sources.name: NIH NICHD Endometriosis Research Program
funding_sources.description: The NIH Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD) supports endometriosis research focused on understanding the biological mechanisms of disease, improving diagnosis, and developing novel treatments. Funds basic, translational, and clinical studies through the standard R01 mechanism.

funding_sources.name: DOD CDMRP Endometriosis Research Program
funding_sources.mechanism: Investigator-Initiated Research Award / Clinical Trial Award
priorities: impact on military readiness and quality of life
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Annually, typically September - November (check CDMRP Program Announcements)
funding_sources.description: The Congressionally Directed Medical Research Programs (CDMRP) Endometriosis Research Program (ERP) funds research to understand, diagnose, and treat endometriosis. Particularly focused on impacts on active-duty service members and veterans. Offers multiple award mechanisms including Idea Awards and Clinical Trials.

funding_sources.name: NIH ORWH Building Interdisciplinary Research Careers in Women's Health (BIRCWH)
priorities: sex and gender influences on health
pain and chronic disease in women
career development in women's health research
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Varies by institution; institutional application typically in spring
funding_sources.description: The NIH Office of Research on Women's Health (ORWH) BIRCWH program provides career development support for early-stage investigators pursuing research in women's health, including endometriosis. Scholars receive mentorship, protected research time, and funding through their host institutions.

funding_sources.name: NIH R21 Exploratory/Developmental Research Grant
funding_sources.amount: Up to $275,000 total direct costs over 2 years
funding_sources.description: The NIH R21 mechanism supports exploratory and developmental research projects that lack sufficient preliminary data for a full R01. Ideal for testing novel concepts or developing new tools and methodologies relevant to endometriosis research. Two-year duration with no renewal.

funding_sources.name: NIH R03 Small Grant Program
funding_sources.amount: Up to $50,000 per year for up to 2 years
funding_sources.description: The NIH R03 Small Grant Program provides limited funding for new investigators to conduct small, self-contained research projects including pilot studies, feasibility studies, and secondary data analyses related to endometriosis. Suitable for researchers building toward larger grants.

funding_sources.name: NIH HEAL Initiative – Endometriosis and Pelvic Pain
priorities: opioid use and misuse in endometriosis patients
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Varies by specific HEAL FOA; check NIH HEAL Initiative website
funding_sources.description: The NIH Helping to End Addiction Long-term (HEAL) Initiative includes funding for endometriosis-related chronic pelvic pain research, particularly focused on understanding pain mechanisms and developing non-opioid treatment alternatives. Supports both basic and translational research.

funding_sources.name: Endometriosis Foundation of America Research Grant
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Typically spring (March - May); check website annually
funding_sources.description: The Endometriosis Foundation of America (EndoFound) funds research aimed at advancing the understanding and treatment of endometriosis, with a particular emphasis on early detection, reducing diagnostic delay, and improving quality of life. Encourages applications from early-career investigators.

funding_sources.name: World Endometriosis Research Foundation (WERF) Grant
priorities: health economics and burden of disease
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Rolling or annual; check WERF website for current opportunities
funding_sources.description: The World Endometriosis Research Foundation (WERF) supports international collaborative research to better understand endometriosis globally. Known for supporting the WERF EPHect (Endometriosis Phenome and Biobanking Harmonisation Project) and multi-site epidemiological studies.

funding_sources.name: PCORI Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Award
priorities: health disparities in diagnosis and treatment
quality of life and functional outcomes
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Varies by cycle; quarterly Letters of Intent deadlines
funding_sources.description: The Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI) funds comparative effectiveness research that prioritizes patient-centered outcomes. Endometriosis research must involve patients as partners and focus on outcomes that matter to patients, such as pain management, fertility, and quality of life.

funding_sources.name: Burroughs Wellcome Fund Career Award for Medical Scientists (CAMS)
funding_sources.description: The Burroughs Wellcome Fund CAMS supports physician-scientists transitioning from postdoctoral training to independent research faculty positions. Relevant for MDs and MD-PhDs studying endometriosis mechanisms with a translational or clinical focus. Highly competitive and prestigious award.

funding_sources.name: Society for Women's Health Research (SWHR) Interdisciplinary Network Grant
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Varies; check SWHR website for current funding opportunities
funding_sources.description: The Society for Women's Health Research (SWHR) supports interdisciplinary research on conditions affecting women, including endometriosis. Their network grants encourage collaboration across disciplines and focus on translating findings into clinical practice and health policy.

funding_sources.name: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Research Award
priorities: clinical research in obstetrics and gynecology
funding_sources.description: ACOG offers research awards and fellowships to support OB/GYN clinician-researchers including those focused on endometriosis. Particularly valuable for residents and fellows seeking to build a research portfolio in gynecologic surgery, reproductive medicine, and endometriosis management.

funding_sources.name: NIH K08 Mentored Clinical Scientist Research Career Development Award
funding_sources.amount: Up to $150,000 per year in salary and research support for up to 5 years
funding_sources.typical_deadline: February 12, June 12, October 12 (standard K award cycles)
funding_sources.description: The NIH K08 award supports early-career physician-scientists with protected time and mentored research experience. Ideal for clinicians (MDs, DOs) pursuing endometriosis research who need dedicated time to develop their independent research program under the guidance of senior mentors.

funding_sources.name: American Society for Reproductive Medicine (ASRM) Research Grant
priorities: assisted reproductive technology outcomes in endometriosis
funding_sources.description: ASRM provides competitive research grants to support reproductive medicine research, including studies on endometriosis and infertility. Grants are available for ASRM members and focus on advancing the understanding of reproductive pathology and treatment options for patients with endometriosis.

funding_sources.name: European Society of Human Reproduction and Embryology (ESHRE) Research Grant
funding_sources.typical_deadline: Typically January - March annually; check ESHRE website
funding_sources.description: ESHRE supports research on human reproduction and embryology, including endometriosis. The society funds studies aligned with their clinical practice guidelines and encourages multi-center European collaborations. ESHRE also supports a dedicated Special Interest Group (SIG) on Endometriosis and Endometrium.
